purpose: CFTP v5 follow-on Finding 5 (MEDIUM) — committed audit trail for the empirical baselines cited in the V=49152 fp16/bf16 numerical-validation tests. Without this artifact the docstring's claimed measurements (mean_loss rel_err 2.47e-8 / 7.37e-9 etc.) have no provenance and the 1e-5 / 1e-4 tolerances cannot be defended against the 'fudged-tolerances-until-green' failure mode.

measurement_context.driver_version_note: measured against the driver shipping with CUDA 13.x — exact build recorded by nsl_cuda_init() at test time

headroom_summary.rationale_for_headroom: The benign-input fixture (x∈U[-0.5,0.5], W∈N(0,0.01)) produces a near-uniform softmax over V=49152 classes — per-tile-max spread ≈ 0, removing the cross-tile-rescale ULP jitter that would dominate at production transformer logit distributions. The current tolerances guard against catastrophic emitter regressions; they do NOT validate the kernel for converged-model logit spreads. See Finding 4 for the production-distribution caveat and the future test extension.

regression_check_recipe: Re-run either test (NSL_SKIP_CUDA_TESTS not set, `--ignored`) and compare the printed rel_err / max_abs values against this baseline. A divergence > 10x merits investigation; > 100x is a regression. The tolerance gates are the absolute backstop, not a substitute for comparing against the baseline.
known_gaps: Production-distribution input (Finding 4): tests should add a second fixture with W∈N(0,0.05) + target-class boost to exercise peaked-softmax regime.

Tests are #[ignore]'d (Finding 5): CI does not run them. A future change should ungate them with NSL_SKIP_CUDA_TESTS=1 honored as no-op, so the SAME baselines are checked on every commit on a Linux/CUDA runner.
